# HUSBAND AND WIFE.

the gospel, priest of any denomination, judges and such justices of the peace as are authorized to unite in matrimony those desiring to marry. To marry must procure a license from the county. If the male is less than 21 years old, or the female less than 18, the consent of the parent or guardian must consent. The person who performs the ceremony of matrimony must, within three months, return the license to the county clerk with a certificate of marriage over his signature.

It is unlawful for a white person to marry a negro. It is also unlawful to marry an idiot or lunatic; or where there is a husband or wife living, unless there has been no divorce, and when the male is under 18 and the female under 12.

(See Age of Consent, Page 7.)

Marriage gives the husband no rights regarding the wife's property, real and personal, which may be held separate in any manner before or after marriage. The wife may be contracted with, and rent her lands, but cannot convey her real estate, unless her husband join in. If the husband dies, the survivor has a one-third interest for life in the real estate the deceased owned in fee simple, and one-half of the personalty. Adultery on the part of either forfeits all rights, unless the parties become reconciled.

# IDIOTS AND LUNATICS.

County courts have jurisdiction within their respective counties over the care of the persons and property of idiots and lunatics and their committees. A committee will not be appointed unless the person charged to be an imbecile is adjudged to be such, and the one so adjudged in person before them. The powers of a committee are the same as the guardian to an infant except that if a person is found to be a lunatic the judge must get up a certificate of the fact and send it to the asylum authorities. A person who can be safely kept in the county may be placed in the care of a person or committee. No allowance is made for any person under the age of 8, or one who is insane at times.

# INHERITANCE TAX.

Property may pass by will or the intestate laws of this state to any person who may die possessed of the same while a resident of this state, or if such deceased person was not a resident of this state at the time of death, which property, or any part of it shall be within this state.

HARVARD UNIVERSITY

to present or take part in any play alleged formerly to exist between race prejudice. A fine of from one to one hundred dollars and imprisonment is imposed for the

**ATTORNEY.**

Instrument by which the power or authority of one person is placed and stead of another as an agent, and the authority to perform specific acts are also employed, such words are necessary to be authorized. The intention of the party in the instrument usually governs the act. Power of attorney to sell or transfer real or personal property may be acknowledged, proved and recorded in the manner prescribed for recording deeds.

**DRINKING CUP.**

A drinking cup on railroad trains, in railroad cars, waiting rooms, depots, restaurants or on steamboats, or other public conveyances that are frequented by people, is prohibited.

In regard to the prohibited use of drinking cups, the owner is subject to a fine of not less than one hundred dollars for each offense, and each day the cup is used is a separate offense.

The owners or agents of such public places shall cause the law posted near the place where the cup is used.

**ROADS.**

Less than seven may associate to the end of constructing and operating a railroad, by articles of incorporation, which shall state the name of the railroad, the number of years the corporation shall exist, the amount of its capital stock, the number of shares, and the amount per share, nor more than fifteen, and their location, the name of each county through which, and the name of each county through which the road is intended to be constructed, and its general route.

The directors shall set opposite to his name the number of shares subscribed by him. If one hundred dollars per mile has been subscribed, the same shall be paid to the directors, and an affidavit
